page: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DDCSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 2458 GB of bundled local NVMe storage and is Premium Storage capable, though it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark data reveals strong multi-core and specialized execution capabilities. The server achieves a Passmark CPU score of 49816.66 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17264.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Cryptographic and vector processing performance are notable, supported by hardware flags like AVX-512 and AES, with Passmark CPU encryption testing reaching 36927.97. Database operations are also efficient, scoring 25047.25 in Passmark database testing, while static web serving throughput reaches 48.38 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is solid, with read-only large block transfers measured at 170.27 GB/sec and write large block transfers at 69.27 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC32ds_v3 provides a dense allocation of memory and local storage relative to its 32 dedicated cores, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of GPU hardware. However, single-core performance remains in the average tier, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1682.0. Large-scale LLM text generation is a clear bottleneck, with the 70B model text generation dropping to a weak 2.64 tokens/sec. Consequently, this server is recommended for memory-heavy databases, enterprise web serving, data compilation, and memory-bound analytical workloads, rather than large-scale AI model generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DDCSv3 family, powered by a 32-core Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17264.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 49816.66. It excels in database operations and static web serving, achieving 48.38 GB/sec in web throughput. However, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak, making it less suitable for heavy AI inference. This server is recommended for memory-intensive databases, enterprise web applications, and data compilation tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC32ds_v3 is a 32-core Intel Xeon server with 256 GB RAM and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core x86 platform featuring high memory density and local storage for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz
256 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
2458 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core execution and database benchmark performance
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and enterprise web serving
